summary: Marked melee/it/items/itheart.c as Matching in configure.py and adjusted several GALE01 symbol entries from global it_* names to local @NN labels so the linked object could match. The decomp.dev report showed linked code increasing by 1744 bytes and linked data by 144 bytes.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itheart.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt for seven data symbols associated with the matching result.
Converted it_803F5718 in .data from a global it_* symbol to local @234 while preserving address 0x803F5718, size 0x25, and data:string.
Converted it_804D5210 and it_804D5218 in .sdata from global it_* string symbols to local @188 and @189.
Converted local .sdata2 float symbols it_804DC8A8 and it_804DC8AC to anonymous local labels @156 and @157.
Converted .sdata2 float symbols it_804DD970 and it_804DD974 from global it_* entries to local @162 and @163.
Bot report for GALE01 reported linked code at 30.67% (+0.04%, +1744 bytes) and linked data at 28.80% (+0.01%, +144 bytes).
smart_moves: The PR did not only flip the Matching flag; it also reconciled symbol-map visibility/name expectations needed for the object to link as matching.
Local data and string constants were represented with @NN labels instead of artificial global it_address names, aligning symbols.txt with local-scope semantics.
The changes were tightly scoped to the matching toggle and the exact symbol entries needed for itheart.
decomp_lessons: When promoting an item source file from NonMatching to Matching, audit the generated or required symbol map for local constants that should not remain exported as global it_* address names.
Matching status changes can require symbols.txt cleanup even when no C source diff is present in the PR slice.
For local data:string and data:float entries, scope and label form can matter as much as address, size, section, and alignment.
A successful matching promotion should be checked against linked code/data deltas from decomp.dev or equivalent build reports.
naming_conventions: Use Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/<file>.c") in configure.py for files that now match.
Reserve it_<address> symbols for global address-named item data where appropriate.
Use @NN anonymous/local labels for local-scope constants in symbols.txt, including local strings and floats.
Keep section, address, size, alignment, and data type comments intact when renaming symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote the object in configure.py only after the object links as matching.
Adjust symbol scope from global to local where the matching object expects local constants.
Rename local .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 constants to @NN labels to avoid forcing global symbol names into the matched object.
Preserve exact addresses and metadata while changing symbol names/scopes, minimizing unintended linker-map churn.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 linked code/data improvements and no additional change notes.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that flip Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) and also rename symbols to @NN local labels.
Search for remaining itheart-related it_* address symbols that may later be named semantically.
Compare itheart.c matching promotion with neighboring item files itcapsule.c, itstar.c, and itbat.c.
Audit symbols.txt local-scope entries that still use it_<address> names instead of @NN labels.
